description:
attached_molecule: th-wisp-pet3
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-03T23:11:09Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Planning phase for full email brand alignment overhaul. Read brand_identity_communications_guide.md, visual_design_system_web_email.md, all existing email specs in emails/, and current templates in app/email/templates/.

Create .specs/plan.md with:
- Project overview (current state vs brand requirements)
- Feature list (numbered 00-06):
  00: Email base template redesign (Georgia serif headlines, black/bone palette, spacing)
  01: Complete email specs 02-14 with brand-aligned copy, tone rules, variants
  02: Rewrite daily cadence template (narrative voice, reduce metadata, add framing)
  03: Rewrite onboarding emails (welcome origin, calibration, first cadence)
  04: Implement milestone emails (birthday, age crossing, count, category, historical)
  05: Fix referral + social email templates for visual consistency
  06: Implement housekeeping emails (preference nudge, re-engage, goodbye)
- Dependency graph
- Complexity score (1-5)

Key brand principles: calm/dry/observational tone, Georgia serif headlines, Black+Bone only, single column 600px, generous whitespace, short declarative sentences, no emojis ever.
notes: Plan created at .specs/plan.md. Current state: 2/15 email specs filled, all templates use wrong fonts (sans-serif instead of Georgia serif), non-brand colors (#666, #444, #e6e6e6), no shared base template, excessive border-radius, hr dividers instead of whitespace. Plan covers 7 features (00-06): base template redesign, complete all 13 empty specs, rewrite daily cadence, rewrite onboarding, implement 5 milestone templates, fix referral/social, implement housekeeping. Overall complexity: 4/5.
title: Plan: Email brand alignment overhaul

close_reason: Already implemented — base.html exists with all required brand elements, all 18 child templates extend it
description: Rebuild HTML email foundation as shared base layout. 600px single-column, Georgia serif headlines, Inter sans-serif body, Black/Bone colors, no border-radius, no hr dividers, flat CTA buttons, mobile responsive. Migrate all existing templates to extend base.
title: Email base template redesign

description:
attached_molecule: th-wisp-jtmn
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-05T19:05:33Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Run the full Ghost Security scan pipeline on this rig's codebase. Execute these three skills in sequence:

1. Invoke /ghost-repo-context — maps the repo structure, detects projects, writes architecture summary to ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/cache/repo.md
2. Invoke /ghost-scan-code — runs AI-powered SAST scan using the context from step 1; covers SQLi, XSS, SSRF, BOLA, path traversal, and other OWASP vectors
3. Invoke /ghost-report — aggregates all scan findings into a prioritized report at ~/.ghost/repos/<repo_id>/scans/<sha>/report.md

After the report is generated, read it and post a summary to the bead notes including: total findings count, high/medium/low breakdown, and top 3 most critical issues found.
notes:
Ghost Security scan complete (quick depth, commit 7bd5615).

RESULTS SUMMARY:
- Total findings: 1
- High: 0, Medium: 1, Low: 0
- Scans run: Code (SAST) — 3 vectors, 9 candidate analyses
- Scans not run: Dependencies (SCA), Secrets

TOP FINDINGS:
1. [MEDIUM] CWE-532: PII (email addresses) logged in plaintext during email change flow (app/routes.py:1156, verify_email_change). Both old and new email addresses are written unmasked to logger.info(). Remediation: mask emails before logging.

No critical or high severity issues found. Authentication (itsdangerous tokens, HTTP Basic admin) and token verification logic passed analysis without findings.

Full report: ~/.ghost/repos/theoutlived-ae4e06df/scans/7bd5615/report.md
title: Security scan: theoutlived

description:
attached_molecule: th-wisp-zo7b
attached_formula: mol-polecat-work
attached_at: 2026-03-05T19:28:48Z
dispatched_by: mayor

Security fix from ghost-scan-code. CWE-532.

In app/routes.py line 1156, the verify_email_change handler logs user email addresses in plaintext:
  logger.info(f"Email changed for user {user.id}: {old_email} -> {new_email}")

Fix: add a _mask_email helper and use it before logging.

def _mask_email(email: str) -> str:
    local, _, domain = email.partition('@')
    return f"{local[0]}***@{domain}" if local else '***'

Replace the logger.info line with:
  logger.info(f"Email changed for user {user.id}: {_mask_email(old_email)} -> {_mask_email(new_email)}")

Also audit the rest of routes.py and any other files for similar plaintext PII logging patterns (user emails, names, etc.) and apply the same masking. Write a unit test for _mask_email.
title: Fix: mask PII emails in logs (theoutlived)

description:
Merge queue processor patrol loop.

The Refinery is the Engineer in the engine room. You process polecat branches, merging them to their effective target branches one at a time with sequential rebasing.

**The Scotty Test**: Before proceeding past any failure, ask yourself: "Would Scotty walk past a warp core leak because it existed before his shift?"

## Merge Flow

The Refinery receives MERGE_READY mail from Witnesses when polecats complete work:

```
Witness                    Refinery                      Git
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGE_READY              │                           │
   │─────────────────────────>│                           │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (verify branch)                   │
   │                          │ fetch & rebase            │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (run tests)                       │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (quality review, if enabled)      │
   │                          │                           │
   │                    (if pass)                         │
   │                          │ merge & push              │
   │                          │──────────────────────────>│
   │                          │                           │
   │ MERGED                   │                           │
   │<─────────────────────────│                           │
   │                          │                           │
```

After successful merge, Refinery sends MERGED mail back to Witness so it can
complete cleanup (nuke the polecat worktree).

## CRITICAL: Read Step Instructions Before Acting

Before executing ANY step, you MUST run `bd show <step-id>` and read the full
description. Steps contain **Config:** values that override default behavior.
If a config says to skip, you skip. If it specifies a command, you use that
exact command — not your own assumption. Do NOT guess what a step requires
based on the step title or your role knowledge. The step description is the
source of truth.

## Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| wisp_type | patrol | Type of wisp created for this molecule |
| integration_branch_refinery_enabled | true | Whether refinery merges to integration branches |
| integration_branch_auto_land | false | Whether to auto-land integration branches when epic children all closed |
| run_tests | true | Whether to run tests before merging |
| setup_command | (empty) | Setup/install command (e.g., `pnpm install`). Empty = skip. |
| typecheck_command | (empty) | Type check command (e.g., `tsc --noEmit`). Empty = skip. |
| lint_command | (empty) | Lint command (e.g., `eslint .`). Empty = skip. |
| test_command | go test ./... | Test command to run (if run_tests is true) |
| build_command | (empty) | Build command (e.g., `go build ./...`). Empty = skip. |
| target_branch | main | Default target branch for merges |
| delete_merged_branches | true | Whether to delete source branches after merge |
| judgment_enabled | false | Enable quality review for merges (true/false) |
| review_depth | standard | Review depth: quick, standard, or deep |

## Target Resolution Rule

When instructions reference `<rebase-target>`, `<merge-target>`, or `<verification-target>`,
resolve that placeholder with this rule:

- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "true": use MR target when present, otherwise main.
- If integration_branch_refinery_enabled = "false": always use main.

## FORBIDDEN Actions

- FORBIDDEN: Landing integration branches to the default branch via raw git commands (`git merge`, `git push`).
  Integration branches may ONLY be landed via `gt mq integration land <epic-id>`.
  This applies regardless of `auto_land` configuration. The pre-push hook enforces this.

## Step Execution Order

You MUST process steps in strict DAG order. Walk through each step sequentially,
unless you are explicitly told to skip to a step.
title: mol-refinery-patrol
